sanskrit:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम्।
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः॥
transliteration:
yeṣāṃ tv anta-gataṃ pāpaṃ janānāṃ puṇya-karmaṇām |
te dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante māṃ dṛḍha-vratāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Those whose sins have come to an end, who are humans of pious deeds—such dual-delusion-freed ones worship Me with firm vow.
contextual_meaning: Only when the inner ledger of guilt is balanced by accumulated life-credit does the mind drop its either-or hallucinations and lock onto steady devotion.
psychological_state: Post-atonement clarity; the calm after the storm of self-reproach, allowing single-pointed commitment.
trigger_scenario: The morning after completing a long-overdue apology or paying off debt—suddenly the mind feels spacious enough to meditate without intrusive guilt.
anchor_text: Good deeds erase guilt, clearing the mind for steady devotion.
